content.cn_text: 现在翻到第7页的第4部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to Section 4 on page 7.

content.cn_text: 第4部分
content.en_text: Section 4

content.cn_text: 你将听到一位大学讲师正在向学生们谈论一份建筑学研究
content.en_text: You will hear a university lecturer talking to his students about an architectural studies

content.cn_text: 作业。
content.en_text: assignment.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第7页和第8页的31到40题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40 on pages 7 and 8.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答31到40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 好的，在今天上午关于公共建筑成功设计的讲座之前，我想提请大家注意
content.en_text: Okay, before this morning's lecture on successful design of public buildings, I want to draw your

content.cn_text: 你们的下一个作业。
content.en_text: attention to your next assignment.

content.cn_text: 所以你们的作业是关于公共纪念碑的，我指的是任何旨在提醒我们重要事件或历史人物的建筑或雕像。
content.en_text: So your assignment is on public monuments and by that I mean any structure or statue

content.cn_text: 旨在提醒我们重要事件或历史人物的建筑或雕像。
content.en_text: that is built to remind us of important events or of historic figures.

content.cn_text: 这份作业应分为三个部分。
content.en_text: The assignment should be divided into three sections.

content.cn_text: 在第一部分开头，你应该确定你正在研究的纪念碑，并说明
content.en_text: At the beginning of Section 1 you should identify the monument you're examining and say

content.cn_text: 你为什么选择它。
content.en_text: why you've chosen it.

content.cn_text: 评论一下你认为这座纪念碑是否成功。
content.en_text: Include a comment on whether you think the monument works.

content.cn_text: 我指的是它的成功之处。
content.en_text: By that I mean its success.

content.cn_text: 你可能觉得它很棒。
content.en_text: You may think it's wonderful.

content.cn_text: 你可能觉得它是一个公共的眼中钉，是人们讨厌看到的东西。
content.en_text: You may think it's a public eyesore, something that people hate looking at.

content.cn_text: 接下来，你还应该在第一部分中包含有关纪念碑建造地点的信息，
content.en_text: Next, you should also include in this first section information about where the monument

content.cn_text: 它的地理位置，以及对其社会重要性的讨论，它对当时人们来说有多
content.en_text: was built, its geographical site, and also a discussion of its social importance, how

content.cn_text: 重要。
content.en_text: relevant it was for the people of the time.

content.cn_text: 你还应该包括它建造的时间、它的历史背景，并且
content.en_text: You should also include information on when it was built, its historical background, and

content.cn_text: 确保你也要适当配图。
content.en_text: make sure you illustrate the assignment appropriately too.

content.cn_text: 我会有这个要求。
content.en_text: I shall expect that.

content.cn_text: 插入你能找到的任何平面图，并确保你使用的所有照片都有图注，并且
content.en_text: Insert any plans you can find and make sure any photographs you use have captions and are

content.cn_text: 在文本中清楚地提及，否则它们将毫无意义。
content.en_text: clearly referred to in the text or they will be meaningless.

content.cn_text: 本节的结尾，你应该包括一些关于设计
content.en_text: To end this section you should include something about the record of the person who designed

content.cn_text: 纪念碑的人的记录，展示该纪念碑与他或她其他作品的关系。
content.en_text: the monument, showing how the monument relates to his or her other work.

content.cn_text: 好的。
content.en_text: Right.

content.cn_text: 在作业的第二部分，你需要详细阐述纪念碑的设计
content.en_text: In section two of the assignment you'll need to give a detailed account of the design

content.cn_text: 和建造过程。
content.en_text: and construction of the monument.

content.cn_text: 首先，我想了解你认为在设计中显而易见的任何特定影响。
content.en_text: Firstly, I'll want to read about any particular influence you think is apparent in the design.

content.cn_text: 通常，其中会有些东西将其与某种既定风格联系起来，
content.en_text: More often than not there will be something in it that links it to an established style,

content.cn_text: 例如古典希腊风格。
content.en_text: classical Greek for example.

content.cn_text: 或者它可能反映了包豪斯或新艺术运动等，你来设计。
content.en_text: Or it may reflect a movement like Bauhaus or Art Nouveau, you design.

content.cn_text: 其次，我想要关于材料选择的信息，不仅是所使用的材料，
content.en_text: Secondly, I want information about the choice of materials, not only those which were used,

content.cn_text: 还有为什么选择它们而不是当时在该地区可用的其他材料的原因
content.en_text: but reasons why they may have been selected from others that were available at the time

content.cn_text: 以及设计师工作地点可用的其他材料的原因。
content.en_text: and in the area where the designer worked.

content.cn_text: 接下来，我还会提到在建造过程中所需的任何特殊技能。
content.en_text: Next, I'll want to mention of any particular skills that were necessary in the construction

content.cn_text: 也是。
content.en_text: too.

content.cn_text: 第三部分是最重要的。
content.en_text: The third section is the most important one.

content.cn_text: 在这里，你将进行更详细的批判性分析。
content.en_text: Here you'll go into much more critical detail.

content.cn_text: 为了补充你自己的评论，你需要在这一部分进行一项调查，使其尽可能
content.en_text: To add to your own comments you'll need to carry out a survey for this section to be

content.cn_text: 全面和完整。
content.en_text: as comprehensive and rounded as possible.

content.cn_text: 问你自己和他人以下问题。
content.en_text: Ask yourself and others the following questions.

content.cn_text: 首先，建造这座纪念碑的理由是否充分？
content.en_text: Firstly, was the reason for erecting the monument a valid one.

content.cn_text: 例如，它是否是普通大众，即街上的普通人，
content.en_text: For example, was it one that ordinary members of the public, the man or woman on the street,

content.cn_text: 能轻易理解的，并且它是否证明了所花费的公共资金的价值？
content.en_text: can readily relate to and did it justify how much public money it cost?

content.cn_text: 它是一笔浪费，还是代表了一项合理的文化投资？
content.en_text: Was it a waste or does it represent a sound cultural investment?

content.cn_text: 然后思考它的情感影响。
content.en_text: Then think about its emotional impact.

content.cn_text: 当人们看着它时，他们有何感受？
content.en_text: When someone looks at it, how do they feel?

content.cn_text: 愤怒、悲伤、无动于衷？
content.en_text: Angry, sad, indifferent?

content.cn_text: 别忘了考虑这座纪念碑与周围环境的契合度。
content.en_text: And don't forget to consider how well the monument sits in its environment.

content.cn_text: 它与环境和谐一致还是格格不入？
content.en_text: Does it harmonize with it or does it clash with it?

content.cn_text: 这重要吗？
content.en_text: And is that important?

content.cn_text: 它能增添效果还是减损效果？
content.en_text: Does it add to the effect or take away from it?

content.cn_text: 好的，这些指导方针应该有帮助。
content.en_text: Right, those guidelines should help.

content.cn_text: 哦，请记住，仔细权衡如何在允许的字数内最大程度地发挥影响力非常重要，
content.en_text: Oh, and remember, it is important to weigh up carefully how to make the maximum impact

content.cn_text: 在这种批判中，在允许的字数内发挥最大影响力。
content.en_text: in this kind of critique within the number of words allowable.

content.cn_text: 你不会因为大量的事实信息而获得额外分数。
content.en_text: You don't get extra marks for lots of factual information.

content.cn_text: 你是因为一篇结构良好的分析而得分的。
content.en_text: You get those for a well-constructed analysis.

content.cn_text: 这才是最重要的，而且这需要基于你的个人意见。
content.en_text: That's what counts, and that needs to be based on your personal opinion.

content.cn_text: 第4部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟的时间检查你的答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 听力考试到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of the listening test.

content.cn_text: 你现在有10分钟时间将答案誊写到听力答题卡上。
content.en_text: You now have 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the listening answer sheet.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

The 34. _____of any particular style on the design

how well the monument suits its 39. _____

Public Monument Assignment
Section one
• Identify the monument and justify your choice
• Comment on the $31$ of the monument
• Add information about its:
• geographical location
• $32$significance
• historical context
• Illustrate assignment with:
• $33$
• photos (with captions)
• End with: record of architect's achiever
Section two: Design and construction
• Include information about
• The $34$of any particular style on the design
• The $35$ used in construction
• Specialist $36$ required
Section three: Assessment of design.
• Content should include:
• personal assessment
• results of your survey
• Use personal assessment and survey to consider
• the $37$ for building the monument
• the monument's $38$ effect on members of the public
• how well the monument suits its $39$
Remember!
• Credit given for good $40$
